pure_concept_samples.question: State the most popular movie? When was it released and who is the director for the movie?
pure_concept_samples.evidence: most popular movie refers to MAX(movie_popularity); when it was released refers to movie_release_year; director for the movie refers to director_name;
pure_concept_samples.SQL: SELECT movie_title, movie_release_year, director_name FROM movies ORDER BY movie_popularity DESC LIMIT 1

pure_concept_samples.question: What is the name of the longest movie title? When was it released?
pure_concept_samples.evidence: longest movie title refers to MAX(LENGTH(movie_title)); when it was released refers to movie_release_year;
pure_concept_samples.SQL: SELECT movie_title, movie_release_year FROM movies ORDER BY LENGTH(movie_popularity) DESC LIMIT 1

pure_concept_samples.question: Name the movie with the most ratings.
pure_concept_samples.evidence: movie with the most rating refers to MAX(SUM(rating_score));
pure_concept_samples.SQL: SELECT movie_title FROM movies GROUP BY movie_title ORDER BY COUNT(movie_title) DESC LIMIT 1

pure_concept_samples.question: For all ratings which are rated in year 2020, name the movies which has the rating scored 4 and above.
pure_concept_samples.evidence: ratings in year 2020 refers to rating_timestamp_utc like '%2020%'; rating_score > = 4;
pure_concept_samples.SQL: SELECT T2.movie_title FROM ratings AS T1 INNER JOIN movies AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id WHERE CAST(SUBSTR(T1.rating_timestamp_utc, 1, 4) AS INTEGER) = 2020 AND CAST(SUBSTR(T1.rating_timestamp_utc, 6, 2) AS INTEGER) > 4

pure_concept_samples.question: For all movies where users left a critic, find the movie name, user, rating and critics comments from the user.
pure_concept_samples.evidence: movies where users left a critic refers to critic IS NOT NULL; critic comments refers to critic;
pure_concept_samples.SQL: SELECT T2.movie_title, T1.user_id, T1.rating_score, T1.critic FROM ratings AS T1 INNER JOIN movies AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id WHERE T1.critic IS NOT NULL

pure_concept_samples.question: What is the percentage of rated movies were released in year 2021?
pure_concept_samples.evidence: percentage = DIVIDE(SUM(movie_release_year = 2021), COUNT(rating_id)) as percent; movies released in year 2021 refers to movie_release_year = 2021;
pure_concept_samples.SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.movie_release_year = 2021 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(*) FROM movies AS T1 INNER JOIN ratings AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id

pure_concept_samples.question: What is the name of the most followed list?
pure_concept_samples.evidence: most followed list refers to MAX(list_followers);
pure_concept_samples.SQL: SELECT list_title FROM lists ORDER BY list_followers DESC LIMIT 1

pure_concept_samples.question: What are the URL to the list page on Mubi of the lists with followers between 1-2 and whose last update timestamp was on 2012?
pure_concept_samples.evidence: URL to the list page on Mubi refers to list_url; list_followers = 1 OR list_followers = 2; last update timestamp was on 2012 refers to list_update_timestamp_utc BETWEEN '2012-1-1' AND '2012-12-31';
pure_concept_samples.SQL: SELECT list_url FROM lists WHERE list_update_timestamp_utc LIKE '2012%' AND list_followers BETWEEN 1 AND 2 ORDER BY list_update_timestamp_utc DESC LIMIT 1

pure_concept_samples.question: When was the first movie released and who directed it?
pure_concept_samples.evidence: first movie refers to oldest movie_release_year;
pure_concept_samples.SQL: SELECT movie_release_year, director_name FROM movies WHERE movie_release_year IS NOT NULL ORDER BY movie_release_year ASC LIMIT 1

pure_concept_samples.question: How many movies were added to the list with the most number of movies? Indicate whether the user was a paying subscriber or not when he created the list.
pure_concept_samples.evidence: list with the most number of movies refers to MAX(list_movie_number); user_has_payment_method = 1 means the user was a paying subscriber when he created the list; user_has_payment_method = 0 means the user was not a paying subscriber when he created the list;
pure_concept_samples.SQL: SELECT T1.list_movie_number, T2.user_has_payment_method FROM lists AS T1 INNER JOIN lists_users AS T2 ON T1.list_id = T2.list_id ORDER BY T1.list_movie_number DESC LIMIT 1

cell_value_samples.question: How many movie lists were created by user 83373278 when he or she was a subscriber?
cell_value_samples.evidence: the user was a subscriber when he created the list refers to user_subscriber = 1; user 83373278 refers to user_id = 83373278;
cell_value_samples.SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM lists_users WHERE user_id = 83373278 AND user_subscriber = 1

cell_value_samples.question: How many films were released in 2007?
cell_value_samples.evidence: film released in 2007 refers to movie_release_year = 2007; film refers to movie
cell_value_samples.SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM movies WHERE movie_release_year = 2007

cell_value_samples.question: How many clients have an email account other than gmail.com?
cell_value_samples.evidence: email account other than gmail.com refers to email not like '%@gmail.com';
cell_value_samples.SQL: SELECT COUNT(email) FROM client WHERE email NOT LIKE '%@gmail.com'

cell_value_samples.question: How many complaints are not in process with an agent?
cell_value_samples.evidence: not in process with an agent refers to outcome ! = 'AGENT';
cell_value_samples.SQL: SELECT COUNT(outcome) FROM callcenterlogs WHERE outcome != 'AGENT'

cell_value_samples.question: How many Credit Card complaints did Sharon handle?
cell_value_samples.evidence: Credit Card refers to Product = 'Credit Card'; Sharon refers to server = 'SHARON';
cell_value_samples.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.`Complaint ID`) FROM callcenterlogs AS T1 INNER JOIN events AS T2 ON T1.`Complaint ID` = T2.`Complaint ID` WHERE T2.Product = 'Credit card' AND T1.server = 'SHARON'

cell_value_samples.question: How many complaints were served in 5 minutes or less by DORIT and responded to the customer with an explanation, were made by phone?
cell_value_samples.evidence: served in 5 minutes or less refers to ser_time < '00:05:00'; DORIT refers to server = 'DORIT'; responded with an explanation refers to "Company response to consumer" = 'Closed with explanation'; made by refers to "Submitted via";
cell_value_samples.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.`Complaint ID`) FROM callcenterlogs AS T1 INNER JOIN events AS T2 ON T1.`Complaint ID` = T2.`Complaint ID` WHERE T1.ser_time < '00:05:00' AND T1.server = 'DORIT' AND T2.`Submitted via` = 'Phone' AND T2.`Company response to consumer` = 'Closed with explanation'

cell_value_samples.question: How many female clients are there older than 30?
cell_value_samples.evidence: female refers to sex = 'Female'; older than 30 refers to age > 30
cell_value_samples.SQL: SELECT COUNT(sex) FROM client WHERE sex = 'Female' AND age > 30

cell_value_samples.question: Please list all first and last names of clients who live in New York city.
cell_value_samples.evidence: New York City refers to city = 'New York City'
cell_value_samples.SQL: SELECT first, last FROM client WHERE city = 'New York City'

cell_value_samples.question: How many animators does Movie No. 129 have?
cell_value_samples.evidence: animators refers to job = 'Animation'; Movie No. 129 refers to movie_id = 129
cell_value_samples.SQL: SELECT COUNT(movie_id) FROM movie_crew WHERE movie_id = 129 AND job = 'Animation'

cell_value_samples.question: List all the keywords with "christmas" in them.
cell_value_samples.evidence: keywords with "christmas" in them refers to keyword_name LIKE '%christmas%'
cell_value_samples.SQL: SELECT keyword_name FROM keyword WHERE keyword_name LIKE '%christmas%'
